sections.paragraph:
Ἀνδρέας, ου, ὁ a Gk. name (Diod S 8, 24; SIG 649, 5; EpArist; Joseph.; a Jew Ἀ. also in Cass. Dio 68, 32, 2) Andrew, brother of Simon Peter; acc. to J 1:44 he was from Bethsaida on the Sea of Galilee, and according to vss. 35, 40 was orig. a disciple of John the Baptist. Mt 4:18; 10:2; Mk 1:16, 29; 3:18; 13:3; Lk 6:14; J 6:8; 12:22; Ac 1:13; GPt 14:60=ASyn. 361, 36; GEb 34, 60f; Papias (2:4).—PPeterson, Andrew, Brother of Simon Peter: NovT Suppl. 1, ’58.—M-M.

Gk. Gk. = Greek

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

EpArist EpArist = Epistle of Aristeas, II b.c.—List 5

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

acc. to acc. to = according to

orig. orig. = original(ly)

GPt GPt = Gospel of Peter—List 1

ASyn. ASyn. = KAland, Synopsis Quattuor Evangeliorum13—Lists 1, 6

GEb GEb = Gospel of the Ebionites—List 1

Papias Papias , early Christian writer, II a.d.—List 1

NovT NovT = Novum Testamentum (periodical)—List 6

Suppl.

Suppl. = Supplement (to serials)

suppl. = supplement, supplied (in grammatical references)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 76
